original_image_tags: A pink paper teletype message from the FBI Communications Section, dated August 6, 1947. The paper has two punched holes at the top and shows signs of being part of a bound file.

elements.description: Typed document on pink paper. Visible text includes header 'FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE COMMUNICATIONS SECTION', a stamp 'AUG 6 1947', a stamp 'TELEMETER', and typed text 'WASHINGTON FROM SFRAN S5 8-6-47 12-09 PM RN'. The body text begins with 'DIRECTOR URGENT FLYING DISCS...' and discusses an investigation. A distribution list is printed on the right edge. Bottom text includes 'RECORD 62-83894-91 F B I 29 SEP 24 1947'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content (pink paper teletype message from FBI, dated August 6, 1947, with punched holes and being part of a bound file), but Description B adds specific quoted text (headers, stamps, body text, distribution list, file number) that Description A omits without contradicting it.
